LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF ALL CARS TIRE SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION ENJOINING DEFENDANT'S USE OF THE DBA "BILL WREN'S TOWING" IS OFF CALENDAR BECAUSE THE MATTER WAS NOT TIMELY SERVED. PLAINTIFF PROVIDED 16 COURT DAYS NOTICE AND SERVED THE PAPERS BY MAIL AND E-MAIL. THE MAIL SERVICE IS UNTIMELY UNDER CCP 1005. THE E-MAIL SERVICE IS ALSO UNTIMELY UNDER CCP 1010.6(A)(4) (2 DAYS ADDED TO ELECTRONIC SERVICE). IF THE MOTION IS REFILED AND SERVED BY EMAIL, THE PAPERS SHOULD REFLECT THAT THERE IS AN AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES TO SERVE BY EMAIL. (CCP 1010.6(A)(2).) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF ALL CARS TIRE SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANT IS ENJOINED FROM USING THE DEBA "BILL WREN'S TOWING" DURING THE PENDENCY OF THE LITIGATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICAHEL MENGARELLI, ESQ., 650.591.2282, FOR PLAINTIFF ALL CARS TIRE SERVICE, INC.
HERNAN CONTRERAS, 415.902.2147, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF ALL CARS TIRE SERVICE, INC.'s Motion For Preliminary Injunction is granted. No opposition filed. Defendant is enjoined from using the deba "Bill Wren's Towing" during the pendency of the litigation. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction Enjoining Deft'S Use Of The Dba "Bill Wren'S Towing"
Off Calendar Mar-12-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ALL CARS TIRE SERVICE, INC.'s Motion For Preliminary Injunction Enjoining Defendant's Use Of The DBA "Bill Wren'S Towing" is off calendar because the matter was not timely served. Plaintiff provided 16 court days notice and served the papers by mail and e-mail. The mail service is untimely under CCP 1005. The e-mail service is also untimely under CCP 1010.6(a)(4) (2 days added to electronic service). If the motion is refiled and served by email, the papers should reflect that there is an agreement between the parties to serve by email. (CCP 1010.6(a)(2).) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
